message:
feat(writing/slack-mattermost): retire stale download CTA + refresh download-button caption

Two related cleanups to the article's treatment of the downloadable
operator primer, both prompted by the previous passes having cut the
markdown guide from 2,575 lines down to 175.

------------------------------------------------------------
 1. Remove the duplicate download CTA at the bottom of the article
------------------------------------------------------------

The big gradient "For operators who want to actually run this" CTA
block that used to sit above the TL;DR sign-off is gone. It was
duplicating the download button that already lives in the hero (first
scroll), and its body was a stale 16-line checklist of "everything
this post skips" — day-zero server ordering, Acme Corp worked example,
Cloudflare click-by-click, SMTP walkthrough, comms kit, resume
playbook, etc. All of that was cut out of both the article and the
primer in the prior de-slopify passes, so the CTA was advertising
contents that no longer exist.

Having the reader see the same download button twice, once in the
hero and once again at the end, was also just noisy. One prominent
placement at the top is cleaner and matches how the other articles on
the site do it.

The `MarkdownDownloadButton` component itself stays (it is still used
in the hero via `<MarkdownDownloadButton />` at the top of the piece);
only the second call site and the CTA wrapper went.

------------------------------------------------------------
 2. Fix the stale caption on the download button
------------------------------------------------------------

Old caption, when the file was ~2,575 lines / 185 KB:

    Download the full guide as Markdown
    for feeding to an AI agent · ~185 KB · 2.5k lines

New caption, now that the primer is short and pitched at "what the
agent reads once before kicking off the skill":

    Download the operator primer as Markdown
    agent-readable handoff · pairs with the three skills

Swaps two things:

- "full guide" → "operator primer". The file is no longer a full
  2.5k-line manual; the per-stage operational detail lives in the
  skills themselves, and the markdown is now a conceptual primer the
  agent reads once before driving the skills.
- "~185 KB · 2.5k lines" → "pairs with the three skills". Absolute
  sizing was specific enough to rot every time the primer is trimmed
  or extended. Framing it as the companion to the three skills gives
  the reader the right mental model and is stable under future edits.

------------------------------------------------------------
 Verified
------------------------------------------------------------

- `bunx eslint components/slack-migration-article.tsx` is clean.
- `bun run build` exits 0 and still prerenders the article as `○`
  (static). The `CheckCircle2` lucide import stays in use (line ~1217
  inside the `What survives the move` section), so no dead-code
  follow-up is needed.

files.patch:
@@ -107,10 +107,10 @@ function MarkdownDownloadButton({ compact = false }: { compact?: boolean }) {

- Download the full guide as Markdown + Download the operator primer as Markdown

- for feeding to an AI agent · ~185 KB · 2.5k lines + agent-readable handoff · pairs with the three skills

@@ -1474,51 +1474,6 @@ claude plugins install slack-migration-to-mattermost-phase-3-ongoing-mattermost- {"If you’re still paying Slack today, at some point in the next eighteen months the vendor will email you a new number, and it will be larger than the current one by some multiple that is too painful to ignore. When it happens, you have two options. You can pay. Or you can open a new Claude Code session, paste one sentence, and be somewhere better by the end of the weekend."}

- {/* Operator-ready source guide CTA */} -
-

- For operators who want to actually run this -

-

- This post is a distilled essay. The 2,500-line source guide (which drives the two skills and which an AI agent reads before kicking off the migration) includes everything this post skips: -

-
    - {[ - "Day-zero server ordering & SSH setup", - "Bootstrap scripts (doctor.sh, bootstrap-tools.sh)", - "MCP worked examples (Slack, Playwright, Mattermost)", - "Full Phase 1 / Phase 2 config.env field tables", - "Stage-by-stage cheatsheet with proof & recovery", - "Troubleshooting index (Phase 1, Phase 2, audit)", - "Printable operator checklist (T−2d, T−1d, T+0, T+7d)", - "User-communications kit (every template, T−7d → T+7d)", - "Legal-approval email template", - "Full credential inventory & rotation cadence", - "Cloudflare & Postmark click-by-click walkthroughs", - "Enterprise Grid split workflow", - "Compliance & audit evidence-pack handoff", - "Resume-after-interrupt playbook per stage", - "jsm install / pin / sync / verify commands", - "Phase 3 maintenance: weekly sweep, restore drill, DR, subagents, scenario pack", - ].map((line) => ( -
  • - - {line} -
    • - ))} -
-
- -

- Paste it into Claude Code or Codex with{" "} - - “read this guide end-to-end, then plan my migration” - {" "} - and the agent drives from there. -

-
-
